title: $collhandle
html:
$collhandle

$collhandle

Returns the handle of the specified entity.

$collhandle { ( Entity ) }{-> $selectedoccs | $clearselection()}

Example

vStruct1 = $collhandle("CUSTOMER")->$selectedoccs

Parameters

Table 1. Parameters
Parameter Data Type Description
Entity String Entity name. If omitted, the current entity is used.
$selectedoccs Struct Built-in attribute that contains a Struct of handles to selected occurrences.
$clearselection String Built-in operation that clears the selection on all the entity occurrences that were selected.

Return Values

Table 2. Values returned in $occhandle
Value Meaning
> 0 Handle of the current occurrence of the entity of Entity.
NULL
  • Entity is an incorrect entity
  • There is no occurrence for the given entity with the name Entity .
  • There are no public operations defined for the entity. If there are no public operations, no signature can be created for it. Without a signature, no handle can be returned; instead a NULL value is returned.
  • An error occurred. $procerror contains the exact error.
Table 3. Values of $procerror commonly returned following $collhandle
Value Error constant Meaning
-1102 <UPROCERR_ENTITY> The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not painted on the component.

Use

Allowed in all component types.

Description

You can use $collhandle to address multiple occurrences of the same entity. This is only possible if you have defined at least one public operation in the Collection Operations container of an entity. Such an operation can be used to process all occurrences of the entity, or selected occurrences.

Addressing Selected Occurrences

When an occurrence is selected, it is added to the collection of selected occurrences. You set or check whether an occurrence is selected using $occhandle->$selected.

The $selectedoccs collection attribute contains a collection of Structs in which each member is a handle to a selected occurrence.

Struct members in a collection can be accessed using Struct Index operator ( vStruct{ N }). To get the occurrence reference, first assign each Struct member to a handle variable, which can then be used to manipulate the individual occurrence.

Example: Getting and Clearing Multiple Selected Occurrences

The following example shows how to get the set of selected occurrences of entity ENT.

  1. Get a Struct of handles to selected occurrences.
  2. Assign each Struct member to a handle variable.
  3. Clear the selection of all occurrences using the $clearselection operation.
Related Links
  • Handles

    A handle is a reference to an object, such as a synchronous component instance, entity, occurrence, or field. Handles can be used to call operations on the referenced object, or to pass these objects as variables and parameters.

  • Structs

    A Struct is a tree-like data structure in memory that is used to dynamically manipulate complex data and transform it from or to XML, JSON, or Uniface component data. A Struct can be addressed by assigning it to a variable, parameter, or non-database field of type struct, and manipulated using ProcScript commands, access operators, and information functions. For information on using Structs, see Transforming Complex Data Using StructsYou can dynamically transform complex data structures, such as XML, JSON, or Uniface component structures, into another format using in-memory data structures called Structs. .

  • Occurrences

    An occurrence is the runtime instance of an entity. Although the entity determines the fields an occurrence may have, and some of its default properties and behavior, each occurrence is unique and contains one set of the fields, whether they contain data or not.

  • Collection Script Container

    Code container of an entity that is used for operations that handle the collection of entity occurrences (as opposed to individual occurrences).

  • $occhandle

    Returns the handle of the current occurrence of the specified entity.

  • $instancehandle

    Return the handle of the requested component instance.
